text:
It wasUSA Basketball 86, Canada 72on Wednesday.脗

Up next: Olympic gold.

Officially, USA-Canada was a meaningless exhibition game. A chance for two North American neighbors to shake the rust off before boarding planes overseas for the real thing. But last summer theCanadians blitzed the U.S. in the bronze medal game of the World Cup. On Wednesday, they waved the white flag early in the fourth quarter.脗

Seriously芒聙聰is anyone competing with this U.S. squad? The game has grown exponentially since the Dream Team days, when Charles Barkley was scrapping with hapless Angolans and Cuban players were posing for pictures on the floor. The field in France is excellent this summer. Nikola Jokic will lead Serbia,Giannis Antetokounmpo will play for Greeceand good luck scoring in the paint on a French team backstopped by Victor Wembanyama and Rudy Gobert.脗

Still, this U.S. team isloaded. Arguably the most talented roster in USA Basketball history. NBA success芒聙聰and the grueling schedule that comes with it芒聙聰haskept Stephen Curry off Olympic rosters. But when LeBron James called last fall to gauge Curry芒聙聶s interest, he jumped at the chance. Shooting was a problem for the 2023 World Cup team. The greatest sniper in NBA history fixes that.脗

To solve a size issue, the U.S. added Joel Embiid, a Cameroonian-born, French/U.S. citizen whose recruitment over the last year felt like free agency. Embiid fouled out in 12 minutes against Canada and will have to adjust to FIBA officiating. But he collected six rebounds in those minutes. With Embiid, Anthony Davis and Bam Adebayo, the U.S. has a center rotation that can match up with anyone.脗

And a roster that should beateveryone. Even with theloss of Kawhi Leonard芒聙聰who will bereplaced by Boston Celtics guard Derrick White芒聙聰the U.S. goes a dozen deep. Ten current All-Stars. Four ex-MVPs. Early in the fourth quarter, Canada emptied its bench. When the U.S. pulled its starters, All-NBA guard Tyrese Haliburton stayed on the floor.脗

It will be choppy at times. What international teams lack in talent they make up for in continuity. This U.S. team has been together for only a few days. In an early scrimmage, they just snuck by aselect team led by a 17-year-old. Against Canada, Team USA scored 14 points in the first quarter before blowing the game open in the second half.脗

芒聙聹We芒聙聶ll get there,芒聙聺 U.S. coach Steve Kerr said. 芒聙聹It芒聙聶s just going to take some time.芒聙聺脗

Time, of course, is not an ally. The U.S. will head to Abu Dhabi this week for exhibitions against Australia and Serbia before a stop in London to play South Sudan and Germany. They will use practices to install a system and games to fine-tune it. In FIBA, the rules are different. On a team filled with All-Stars, roles will be, too.脗

芒聙聹We focus on what we know is going to beat us in a FIBA game,芒聙聺 Kerr said. 芒聙聹I芒聙聶m not going to jump on the guys for a mistake that they might make. Dribbling the ball out of bounds, making a bad pass. I芒聙聶m going to jump on them for FIBA mistakes.

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s a different game entirely. The rules are different, the rhythm, the flow. All these players are used to getting 20 shots per game. They might only get five or six. They have to get acclimated to FIBA and to this style 芒聙娄 trying to get them to understand what wins and what loses a FIBA game.芒聙聺

Wednesday芒聙聶s exhibition was Team USA芒聙聶s first chance to learn. The ball was moving in the first quarter芒聙聰maybe a little too much. 芒聙聹A littletoounselfish,芒聙聺 noted USA Basketball managing director Grant Hill. Possessions looked disjointed. The uncertainty that comes with playing with new faces was plainly evident. The U.S. committed 15 turnovers and shot just 30.4% from three.

Said Davis, 芒聙聹Offensively we were, to keep it frank, pretty terrible.芒聙聺

When they had to dial it up, though芒聙聰yeesh. Defense will be this team芒聙聶s calling card. The interior muscle of Embiid and Davis. The versatility of Jrue Holiday, Anthony Edwards and (eventually) White. The intellect of James. Canada, among the non-U.S. contenders for gold, shot 33.8%. The U.S. had 11 steals and nine blocks.脗

芒聙聹That芒聙聶s where we芒聙聶re going to hang our hat,芒聙聺 Embiid said. 芒聙聹And offensively it芒聙聶s going to come.芒聙聺

It will. The U.S. has too much firepower. Curry connected on three of his six three-point attempts. Edwards scored 13 off the bench. Jayson Tatum (eight points) had some strong drives to the rim. Meanwhile, Team USA芒聙聶s all-time leading scorer (Kevin Durant, who is resting a minor calf injury) watched the game in street clothes.脗

There are things to clean up, sure. But no reason to believe they won芒聙聶t.脗

芒聙聹I芒聙聶ve got to coach them,芒聙聺 Kerr said. 芒聙聹They have got to get better, they know that. We have got to get better as a staff. Every day we are focused on that.芒聙聺脗

Chris Mannix is a senior writer at Sports Illustrated covering the NBA and boxing beats. He joined the SI staff in 2003 following his graduation from Boston College. Mannix is the host of SI芒聙聶s 芒聙聹Open Floor芒聙聺 podcast and serves as a ringside analyst and reporter for DAZN Boxing. He is also a frequent contributor to NBC Sports Boston as an NBA analyst. A nominee for National Sportswriter of the Year in 2022, Mannix has won writing awards from the Boxing Writers Association of America and the Pro Basketball Writers Association, and is a longtime member of both organizations.

title: Even With Lack of Time Together, USA Men芒聙聶s Basketball Shows It Can Beat Anyone
description: There are things to clean up over the next few exhibition games, but a team with this much firepower still has the inside track to win Olympic gold.
